description: A naturally occurring hard mass or piece of mineral matter is 'kerou'. Stone and rock overlap in ordinary use, with size and setting supplied by the scene. 'muila' (earth) covers soil or ground; 'kirae' (crystal) selects an ordered internal structure.
articulatory_notes: Unvoiced 'k' begins with a brief closure between the back of the tongue and the soft palate, then releases into front 'e'. A tap or brief trill enters stressed rounded 'o', followed by a clear hiatus into high-back rounded 'u'.
usage_notes: A count can insert optional 'themo' before one stone; building material needs none. A geological name can supply mineral identity when it matters.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Local stone teaches through fracture, grain, weathering, and weight. A wall stone, a sharpening stone, and a carving stone may come from the same hillside and ask different things of the hand.

semantic_domains.creation: mineral material selected for building, tools, or carving
semantic_domains.nature: naturally occurring rock or mineral matter
semantic_domains.physical: a hard mineral mass or bounded piece
